name: municipal_services_cross_border_data_harbor_jus_mer
premise: A Municipal Services response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving municipal services cross border data harbor jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Keisha Patel
agents.identity: I am Keisha Patel, accountable for a defensible call in Municipal Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Luc Bennett
agents.identity: I am Luc Bennett, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Municipal Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Keisha Patel must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Keisha Patel do?
